title: vicia sericocarpa subsp. microphylla
intro: Vicia sericocarpa subsp. microphylla, also known as the smallflower pea or small-flowered vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family. It is native to Australia and has been used by indigenous cultures for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. The plant's leaves, seeds, and roots contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health conditions, including wounds, skin conditions, and urinary tract infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal infusions, decoctions, and poultices for medicinal use, although modern preparations may also include extracts and capsules.
